appendix.title: TemporalParquet: a Parquet Footer Convention for Temporal Types
appendix.para: Apache Parquet has become the de-facto interchange format for columnar analytics data on object storage. Geospatial data has had a documented Parquet convention — GeoParquet — since 2022; mobility data (trajectories, temporal types, spans, sets) has not. This appendix documents TemporalParquet, the convention MobilityDB adopts for storing temporal columns in Parquet files: a BYTE_ARRAY column carrying MEOS-WKB encoded values, with a JSON metadata object in the Parquet file footer that describes each column's encoding, base type, subtype, and (where applicable) SRID, geodetic flag, or H3 resolution.

The convention is the file-format substrate of the Temporal Data Lake architecture (RFC #912) and is consumed in production by MobilityDuck (DuckDB extension) and MobilitySpark (Apache Spark binding). The same .parquet shard written by one platform reads back losslessly on the other two.

sect1.title: File Structure
sect1.para: For every column carrying a MobilityDB temporal type, the column is BYTE_ARRAY with logical-type NONE; each row value is the MEOS-WKB encoding of the temporal value; NULLs are encoded as Parquet NULLs.
The Parquet file's key_value_metadata carries an entry with key temporal whose value is a JSON document:
The temporal object coexists with GeoParquet's geo object: a single file can have both.

sect1.title: Type Coverage

row.entry: First cross-platform extended type. Carries optional h3_resolution; see .
tcbuffer, tnpoint, tpose, trgeo, tpcpoint, tpcpatch
Remaining extended temporal types; bindings ship in subsequent releases following the th3index reference shape.

sect1.para: The subtype field applies only to lifted temporal types (Instant / Sequence / SequenceSet); span / set / box columns omit it.

sect2.title: Type-Specific Optional Fields
sect2.para: Some base_type values may carry optional metadata that lets consumers decide whether the column is usable for a given workload without decoding any row:

listitem.para: srid — EPSG code of the column's CRS. Required for spatial-temporal types.
geodetic — true ⇒ spheroidal-metre math (Haversine / Vincenty); see .
has_z — column carries a Z dimension throughout.

h3_resolution — applies to th3index. Optional integer in [0, 15] declaring every cell in the column was produced at this resolution. Consumers MAY rely on it to gate cell-membership prefilters (cross-join probes via everEqH3IndexTh3Index) without decoding any row.

sect2.title: Geodetic Distances
sect2.para: tgeompoint stores coordinates in the input CRS (e.g. lon/lat WGS-84) and computes Euclidean distances in that coordinate space; length(tgeompoint) over a WGS-84 trajectory therefore returns degrees, not kilometres.

tgeogpoint is the geodetically-correct variant: it carries the same MEOS-WKB bytes but with the geodetic flag set in the type tag, causing MEOS to route all spatial math through the spheroidal Haversine / Vincenty engine — lengths and speeds are in metres.

When writing TemporalParquet files that consumers will use for distance or speed analytics, prefer tgeogpoint (set "geodetic": true in the column metadata). The geodetic flag is self-describing in MEOS-WKB: a file written with asBinary(tgeogpointSeq(...)) reconstructs as a geodetic sequence on any platform that calls tgeogpointFromBinary(blob).

sect1.title: Worked Example: th3index
sect1.para: th3index is the first extended temporal type to ship a cross-platform binding pair. The MEOS-WKB encoder handles the th3index payload identically to tbigint (both lift over an int64); the metadata base_type is the discriminator. The companion h3_resolution field declares uniform resolution per column so consumers can gate cell-membership prefilters without scanning the data.
Example footer entry for a BerlinMOD trips table:

The companion trip_h3 column is produced once at load time via tgeompoint_to_th3index(trip, 7) and acts as a cheap cell-membership prefilter on subsequent cross-join queries that runs unchanged on MobilityDB, MobilityDuck and MobilitySpark:
sect1.programlisting: -- BerlinMOD Q5 (nearest-approach) with the th3index prefilter SELECT t1.licence AS licence1, t2.licence AS licence2, nearestApproachDistance(t1.trip, t2.trip) AS d FROM Trips t1 JOIN Trips t2 ON t1.vehId < t2.vehId WHERE everEqTh3IndexTh3Index(t1.trip_h3, t2.trip_h3) AND nearestApproachDistance(t1.trip, t2.trip) IS NOT NULL;
sect1.para: interpolation is always step for th3index (a vehicle is in exactly one cell at a time); subtype follows the source tgeompoint's subtype after the lifted conversion. The h3_resolution field is the consumer-side hint that lets a reader gate the prefilter without decoding any row.

note.title: Soundness note (2026-05-11)
note.para: The current tgeompoint_to_th3index implementation samples one cell per source instant; cells traversed by the trip's straight-line segment between consecutive instants are not visited. Until the fix/th3index-srid-flags-lift branch and the prefilter-soundness follow-ups land, trip_h3 is an under-sampling of the true cell set, and any prefilter built on it may miss true hits.

The TemporalParquet metadata schema is unaffected — base_type: "th3index" and h3_resolution describe the bytes regardless of how the column was produced. Consumers who need strict semantic correctness must run the underlying tgeompoint predicate without relying on the prefilter as a WHERE clause; treat the prefilter as a future optimisation tracked by the th3index port chain (MobilityDB PRs #807, #866, #938 plus the pending fix branch).

sect1.title: Encoding Versioning
sect1.para: encoding_version is MAJOR.MINOR of the MEOS-WKB schema. New WKB tags (e.g. when a future temporal type lands) bump MINOR; breaking layout changes bump MAJOR. Readers MUST refuse files with a higher MAJOR than they support; readers MAY accept higher MINOR by skipping the unrecognised tags.

sect1.title: Reference Implementation
sect1.para: A Python reference implementation ships in scripts/parquet/ (RFC POC PR #831): tp_export.py writes annotated TemporalParquet files, tp_import.py reads them back, and tp_inspect.py dumps the footer metadata. A round-trip regression test lives at mobilitydb/test/io/queries/090_temporalparquet_roundtrip.test.sql.
The convention spec itself, beta-testing guide, and the cross-platform port history live in doc/temporal-parquet/; this appendix is the user-facing summary.

listitem.para: RFC #912 — Temporal Data Lake (edge-to-cloud architecture; this convention is the file-format substrate).
GeoParquet specification — the spatial-Parquet standard this convention is modelled on.
